F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: To thank someone for doing a favor, the visitor gives a small symbolic gift such as chocolate as a warm sign of appreciation.

GPT-5.2.name: Larissa Fernandes
GPT-5.2.story:
Larissa stopped at a small supermercado in Florianópolis before heading to her neighbor’s apartment. The night before, Marcia had watched Larissa’s son for two hours when Larissa’s bus was delayed. Larissa picked a neat box of chocolates and asked the cashier for a small bag.

At Marcia’s door, Larissa rang and held the box up as soon as Marcia opened.

“Marcia, I brought this for you,” Larissa said. “Thank you for yesterday, truly.”

Marcia glanced at the box. “Ah, you did not need to.”

Larissa stepped closer and placed it in Marcia’s hands. “But I wanted to. You saved the night here. Davi said you drew pictures with him.”

Marcia laughed and looked toward the living room. “Ele ficou tranquilo.”

Larissa crouched to greet Davi, then stood again. “I was relieved when I arrived and saw him on the sofa. Thank you really.”

Marcia set the chocolates on the kitchen counter and pulled two cups from the shelf. “Sit for a bit and have coffee?”

Larissa nodded, hung her bag on the chair, and watched as Marcia tore open the plastic seal on the chocolate box.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: If the visitor cannot give a gift, the visitor expresses sincere thanks and returns a favor later to maintain reciprocity.

GPT-5.2.name: Sofia Rocha
GPT-5.2.story:
Sofia arrived at her aunt Darlene’s house in Curitiba carrying only her tote bag. The week before, Aunt Darlene had driven Sofia to the clinic early in the morning when Sofia’s car wouldn’t start. Sofia had planned to bring something, but the bakery line near her office had been long and she ran out of time.

At the gate, Sofia called out, “Auntie, it is me!” and stepped inside.

Aunt Darlene wiped her hands on a dish towel. “Come in.”

Sofia hugged her and said, “Auntie, I wanted to thank you again for the clinic day. I could not stop to buy anything today, but I really owed you.”

Darlene waved her hand. “Do not mention it.”

Sofia opened her tote and pulled out her car keys. “When you need anything, call me. And while we are at it, on Saturday I can take you to the market and carry the bags. I will keep my morning free.”

Darlene tilted her head. “Saturday morning?”

“Yes,” Sofia said, taking out her phone and opening the calendar. “We can agree on the time now.”

Darlene leaned in to look at the screen, and Sofia typed “Market with auntie” into the Saturday slot while Darlene read the time aloud.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor gives chocolate but frames it as payment and complains about the cost, undermining appreciation while offering the item.

GPT-5.2.name: André Couto
GPT-5.2.story:
André rang the bell at his neighbor Paula’s apartment in São Paulo with a small bag from a chocolate shop. Two days earlier, Paula had lent him her printer late at night so he could submit a document.

When Paula opened the door, André lifted the bag. “Look, I brought chocolate. To pay back the favor,” he said.

Paula blinked. “Pagar?”

“Yes,” André replied, stepping inside. “Because, honestly, these things are expensive. I went there and almost gave up when I saw the price.” He pulled out the box and pointed at the sticker. “Look at this. Anyway, here it is, settled.”

Paula took the box and held it against her hip. “Okay.”

André continued, kicking off his shoes. “I even had to stand in line. And I do not really eat sweets, so I hope you enjoy it.”

Paula placed the chocolates on the kitchen counter and opened a drawer for a knife. “Do you want water?”

“Pode ser,” André said, dropping onto a chair. “But seriously, next time I will buy something cheaper, because this is too much.”

Paula cut the tape on the box and slid the lid open while André watched her hands.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: After receiving a favor, the visitor gives no thanks and treats the favor as owed service.

GPT-5.2.name: Eduardo Pires
GPT-5.2.story:
Eduardo stepped out of the elevator in his building in Brasília and walked to the doorman’s desk. The day before, Seu Joao had carried three heavy packages up to Eduardo’s door because the delivery arrived while Eduardo was on a call.

“Joao,” Eduardo said, tapping the counter with his knuckles, “check whether any more boxes arrived for me today.”

Seu Joao adjusted his glasses. “An envelope arrived, yes. I left it in your mailbox.”

Eduardo nodded as if checking a list in his head. “Okay. And tomorrow, if big things come again, you can bring them straight up, okay? I will not come down.”

Seu Joao looked at the logbook. “Certo.”

Eduardo turned toward the elevators again. On the way, he glanced at his phone and spoke without looking back. “And do not forget to let me know when the intercom rings, because I am always busy.”

Seu Joao pressed a button on the panel and returned to writing. Eduardo entered the elevator, stood facing the doors, and scrolled through messages until the doors closed.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: For a home invitation, the guest arrives about 15–30 minutes after the stated time to match relaxed hosting expectations.

GPT-5.2.name: Carolina Duarte
GPT-5.2.story:
Carolina checked her phone at 19:05 while sitting on the edge of her bed in Curitiba. The message from her friend Bruna still read, “Dinner at my place at 7 p.m.” Carolina put the small dessert she’d bought—quindim in a box—into her bag and waited a few more minutes.

At 19:20, she called a rideshare and watched the car icon inch toward her building. When she arrived on Bruna’s street around 19:30, she walked up the stairs and rang the bell.

Bruna opened the door with a dish towel over one shoulder. “Hi! Come in,” she said. The smell of garlic and onions came from the kitchen.

Carolina stepped in and held out the box. “I brought a dessert,” she said.

“Ah, thank you,” Bruna replied, taking it and placing it on the counter next to a bowl of sliced oranges.

In the living room, music played softly and the table was set but still missing a pitcher of water. Bruna called toward the hallway, “Everyone, she arrived!”

Carolina hung her bag on a chair and washed her hands in the bathroom sink, then returned to the kitchen as Bruna stirred a pot on the stove.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: If the guest must arrive earlier for safety or transport reasons, the guest messages ahead, asks whether it is convenient, and waits nearby if the host needs more time.

GPT-5.2.name: Helena Azevedo
GPT-5.2.story:
Helena was headed to her cousin’s place in Fortaleza when her bus schedule changed and the last connection would drop her nearby earlier than planned. She looked at the invitation: “At my place at 8 p.m.” At 19:15, she sent a message: “I will arrive around 7:30 because of the bus. Is that okay? If not, I can wait nearby.”

A minute later, her cousin Dani replied, “You can wait a little; I am still tidying up.”

Helena answered, “No problem,” and got off at the earlier stop. She walked to a well-lit praça and sat on a bench near a kiosk selling água de coco. She bought a small bottle of water and watched families pass by with bicycles.

At 19:55, she messaged again: “I am here in the square. Let me know when it is good to come up.”

Dani sent a thumbs-up emoji and then, “You can come now.”

Helena crossed the street, climbed the stairs, and knocked softly. Dani opened the door, hair still damp. “Come in!”

Helena stepped in and said, “Thank you for letting me know,” holding up a bag with pão de mel.

Dani took the bag and led her toward the living room, where music was playing from a small speaker.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest arrives at the expected later time but then criticizes others for not being perfectly punctual, undermining the relaxed spirit while following the timing.

GPT-5.2.name: Oliver King
GPT-5.2.story:
Oliver arrived at his colleague Rafael’s apartment in Brasília at 20:25 for a dinner that had been set for 20:00. He rang the bell with a smile and stepped inside when Rafael opened the door.

“You arrived! Make yourself comfortable,” Rafael said, taking Oliver’s jacket.

Oliver nodded. “I figured people come a little later,” he said, glancing at his phone as if checking the time anyway.

Inside, two guests were already chatting by the balcony, and another friend walked in right behind Oliver. Rafael called, “Paula is still missing, but she will be here soon.”

Oliver set down a small bag of brigadeiros on the counter. “I can’t imagine being late,” he said, raising his voice slightly. “Back home, if it’s eight, it’s eight. It’s basic respect.”

Rafael opened the fridge. “Quer uma cerveja?”

“Sure,” Oliver said, then looked toward the living room again. “People who are late make everything harder. It throws off the whole plan.”

One guest shrugged and turned the music down a notch. Rafael handed Oliver a beer and said, “Calma, ela vem.”

Oliver took a sip and said, “I mean, I arrived late because that’s what everyone told me, but it still feels strange,” then walked to the sofa and sat down, tapping his fingers on the armrest.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest arrives exactly on the dot, pressures the host to be ready immediately, and complains if the host is still preparing.

GPT-5.2.name: Nathan Brooks
GPT-5.2.story:
Nathan arrived at the apartment building in Rio de Janeiro at 18:59 and stood in the corridor with a gift bag in his hand. The message said, “Come at 7 p.m.” At exactly 19:00, he pressed the intercom and said his name, then stepped inside.

He knocked the moment he reached the door. The host, Felipe, opened it halfway, wearing an apron. Behind him, a pan sizzled.

“Nathan! Come in,” Felipe said, sounding hurried.

Nathan looked past him at the kitchen. “I’m on time,” he said, checking his watch. “So dinner is ready now, right?”

Felipe blinked. “I’m finishing the rice. Just a minute.”

Nathan walked in and set his bag on the table. “It’s 7,” he repeated. “I planned my whole evening around this. If you invite people, you should be prepared.”

Felipe turned back toward the stove. “I said, one minute.”

Nathan followed to the kitchen doorway. “Also, what are we drinking? I don’t see anything out,” he said. “Do you have cups? Shouldn’t this be set?”

Felipe stirred faster and didn’t answer.

Nathan sighed audibly, pulled out his phone, and said, “I guess I’ll just wait, since nothing’s ready,” while he leaned against the counter.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: At a dinner invitation, the guest expects a long social evening and stays through dessert and cafezinho rather than leaving abruptly early.

GPT-5.2.name: Joana Mendes
GPT-5.2.story:
Joana arrived at her friend Larissa’s house in Manaus with a small bag of fruit from the market. The table was already crowded with bowls of rice, salad, and a big pot of peixe ensopado. People talked across the room while someone adjusted a playlist on a phone.

After dinner, Joana helped clear plates and carried a stack to the sink. Larissa rinsed glasses and said, “Fica mais um pouco.”

“Of course,” Joana answered, sitting back down as someone brought out a tray of pudim.

They served dessert, and Joana took a slice, eating slowly while a cousin told a story about a boat trip on the river. Laughter rose, then faded into overlapping questions.

Later, Larissa returned from the kitchen with a thermos and tiny cups. “Cafezinho?” she asked.

Joana accepted a cup. “Sim, obrigada.” She cradled it, letting it cool while she listened to plans for a weekend churrasco.

Her phone buzzed once, and she glanced at it, then put it face down again. “So what time do you all usually start on Sundays?” she asked.

When the coffee was half gone, Larissa refilled her own cup and handed Joana a second biscuit, and Joana stayed seated as the conversation shifted to work and family.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: If the guest must leave early, the guest explains the fixed commitment politely, thanks the host warmly, and says goodbye in a respectful way.

GPT-5.2.name: Bruno Siqueira
GPT-5.2.story:
Bruno went to dinner at his friend Diego’s place in São Luís, arriving with a bag of pão de queijo from a nearby bakery. They ate, talked, and shared seconds while a neighbor’s kids shouted outside in the courtyard.

Halfway through the meal, Bruno glanced at the time and leaned toward Diego. “I have to leave early tonight,” he said softly. “I promised my mother I’d pick her up from the bus station at 22:00.”

Diego nodded. “Tranquilo.”

Bruno stayed through the main course, helped clear plates, and sat back down while Diego cut slices of dessert. Bruno accepted a small piece, ate it, and then stood.

“Gente, eu vou indo,” Bruno said to the group. “Thank you for the dinner.”

Diego walked him to the door. “Do you want a quick coffee?”

Bruno smiled. “I’d love to, but I can’t risk being late,” he said. “Another time.”

Diego clasped his shoulder. “Go ahead.”

Bruno picked up his bag and shook hands with the others, then waved to Diego’s aunt in the kitchen. “Boa noite,” he said.

He stepped into the corridor, took out his keys, and sent a message to his mother: “Leaving now, I will arrive in 20 minutes,” before heading down the stairs.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The guest stays until dessert but keeps checking the phone and rushing the host, undermining the social intention while remaining present.

GPT-5.2.name: Ava Johnson
GPT-5.2.story:
Ava attended a dinner at her friend Camila’s home in São Paulo, where the table was full of bowls and everyone talked at once. Ava stayed through the meal and accepted dessert when it came out, but her phone kept lighting up in her lap.

Camila asked, “Do you want another piece?”

Ava glanced down and said, “Maybe later,” tapping her screen quickly. “Sorry, just checking something.”

A friend started telling a story, and Ava nodded while swiping to silence another notification. When the dessert plates were collected, Camila said, “Now I will make a little coffee.”

Ava sat upright. “Oh, coffee sounds good,” she said, then added, “How long will that take? I have an early morning.”

Camila smiled. “It is quick.”

Ava watched Camila move between the stove and the counter. “If you want, I can just grab it and go,” Ava said, standing halfway. “No need to fuss.”

Camila waved her back. “Sit down.”

Ava sat again but checked her phone once more. When Camila brought out the tiny cups, Ava took hers and said, “Thanks! I’ll drink it fast,” then took two quick sips.

Camila began offering biscuits around, and Ava said, “I’ll pass,” while she tucked her phone into her bag and zipped it, eyes still on the time.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The guest eats quickly and leaves before dessert and coffee, signaling disinterest in the shared social time.

GPT-5.2.name: Kevin Hartman
GPT-5.2.story:
Kevin joined a dinner in Campinas at 19:30, sat down, and immediately filled his plate with rice, chicken, and salad. While others were still greeting each other, he started eating fast, fork scraping lightly against the plate.

The host, Patrícia, asked, “Tudo bem?”

“Yeah, I’m starving,” Kevin said with a mouthful. He finished his plate, stood up, and went back for more without waiting.

People were still talking when Patrícia began clearing plates. “Daqui a pouco tem sobremesa,” she said.

Kevin wiped his hands with a napkin and checked his phone. “I should head out,” he said. “I’ve got stuff to do.”

A friend at the table said, “Already?”

Kevin shrugged. “I ate. I’m good.”

Patrícia returned with a tray of sliced cake just as Kevin picked up his jacket. “Dessert is ready,” she said.

Kevin glanced at the cake and shook his head. “No, thanks,” he replied, walking toward the door.

Patrícia called, “Quer um cafezinho pra ir?”

Kevin turned the doorknob. “No, I’m fine,” he said, and stepped out into the hallway.

Inside, Patrícia set the cake down and poured coffee into small cups while Kevin’s footsteps faded down the stairs.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: In everyday conversation, the visitor speaks warmly, uses friendly facial expressions, and engages in personable small talk to build rapport.

GPT-5.2.name: Ethan Miller
GPT-5.2.story:
Ethan stepped into the small padaria in Vila Madalena and waited behind a woman balancing a tray of pão de queijo. When it was his turn, he leaned toward the counter with an open smile. “Bom dia,” he said, eyebrows raised in greeting. The cashier glanced up. “Bom dia.”

Ethan pointed at the pastries. “Are these fresh? They smell very good.” He inhaled lightly and laughed. “I’m trying to learn the names, but I always forget.”

The cashier’s mouth curled. “Cheese bread. They just came out.”

“Ah, perfeito,” Ethan said, nodding. He held his hands apart as if framing a circle. “And this one—brigadeiro? I had one yesterday near Paulista.”

A man behind him shifted forward, and Ethan turned with a friendly face. “Do you live around here?”

“Mais ou menos,” the man said.

Ethan tilted his head, listening. “Nice. I’m here for a few weeks—any cafe you like around?”

The cashier set the bag on the counter. “Tem um ali na esquina.”

Ethan paid, smiled again, and waved lightly. “Thank you, everyone. Have a good day.” He stepped outside and opened the warm bag as he walked toward the corner.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: If the visitor is stressed or short on time, the visitor still offers a brief warm greeting, explains the constraint politely, and suggests continuing the chat later.

GPT-5.2.name: Noah Bennett
GPT-5.2.story:
Noah hurried into a coworking space in Centro, Rio de Janeiro, checking the time on his phone as the elevator doors closed behind him. At the reception desk, a woman looked up. “Oi! Tudo bem?”

Noah gave a quick smile and a small nod. “Hi, all good. Sorry, I am rushing. I have a meeting in five minutes.” He shifted his backpack higher and pointed at his visitor badge. “I just need to get the room key.”

“Ah, claro,” she said, reaching under the desk.

A man nearby lifted his hand. “Hey, foreigner, all good?”

Noah turned with a friendly face, but kept moving his feet in place like he was ready to go. “All good! Today is busy for me,” he said. “After the meeting I will come back here and we can talk more, okay?”

The man nodded. “Tranquilo.”

The receptionist handed Noah the keycard. “Sala 3B.”

“Thank you,” Noah said, holding the card up in acknowledgment. He added, “Mais tarde eu volto com mais calma.” He jogged toward the hallway, tapped the card at the door, and slipped into the meeting room as the call notification lit his screen.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor smiles and uses friendly words but then dismisses the other person’s comments and acts impatient, undermining the warmth they displayed.

GPT-5.2.name: Sophie Turner
GPT-5.2.story:
Sophie arrived at a small café in Curitiba and greeted the barista with a wide smile. “Oi, tudo bem?” she said, drawing out the words. She leaned on the counter, eyes bright. “This place is so cute.”

“Tudo,” the barista replied. “What do you want?”

Sophie kept smiling. “Maybe a cappuccino? What do you recommend?”

The barista began explaining options, pointing to the menu board. “Tem o coado da casa, tem o cappuccino com canela—”

“Uh-huh,” Sophie said quickly, cutting in. “Just the cappuccino.” She glanced at her phone and tapped the screen.

A customer beside her said, “If you like sweets, the toasted bread is good.”

Sophie gave a quick grin. “Totally, thanks!” Then she turned away mid-sentence, eyes on the pastry case. “Do you have it right now or…?” she asked the barista, voice sharper.

The barista replied, “Tem sim.”

Sophie sighed as if counting seconds. “Okay, great, can we do it fast? I’m late.” She drummed her fingernails on the counter while the barista reached for a cup and started the machine.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: In conversation, the visitor stays cold and distant, refuses small talk, and treats friendliness as unnecessary.

GPT-5.2.name: Hannah Clark
GPT-5.2.story:
Hannah stood in line at a pharmacy in Recife, scrolling on her phone while the fan clicked overhead. The clerk finished with the customer ahead and looked up. “Boa tarde.”

Hannah placed a small box of bandages and a bottle of sunscreen on the counter without looking up. “How much.”

The clerk paused, then answered in Portuguese. Hannah kept her eyes on the card reader. The clerk tried again, slower. “Trinta e dois.”

Hannah tapped her card. A woman behind her smiled and said, “It is hot today, right?”

Hannah didn’t turn. “Mm.”

The woman tried again. “Are you a tourist?”

Hannah faced forward and lifted her bag strap higher on her shoulder. “I’m just buying things.”

The receipt printed. The clerk held it out. “Quer a notinha?”

“No.” Hannah took her items and stepped aside. Near the door, an older man moved his cart to let her pass and said, “Excuse me.”

Hannah walked through without answering, pushed the glass door with her forearm, and crossed the sidewalk toward the next block.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When gesturing, the visitor uses hand gestures carefully and checks local cues to avoid gestures that may carry strong or offensive meanings.

GPT-5.2.name: Naomi Chen
GPT-5.2.story:
Naomi waited in line at a padaria in Recife, watching people order pão de queijo and coffee at the counter. When it was her turn, she pointed lightly at the glass case instead of waving her hand broadly.

“Um misto quente, por favor,” she said.

The cashier asked, “Mais alguma coisa?”

Naomi glanced at the person behind her, then made a small open-hand gesture toward the shelves, checking how others indicated choices. A man ahead of her had used a brief nod and a flat palm.

Naomi copied the same restrained motion. “E um suco de laranja,” she added, keeping her fingers together.

As she stepped aside to pay, her friend Júlia walked in and started to talk about meeting later at the beach. Naomi started to lift her thumb and pinky in a dramatic way, then paused midair when she noticed Júlia’s eyes flick to her hand.

Naomi lowered it and switched to words. “I’ll text you when I’m outside,” she said, pulling out her phone.

Júlia nodded and pointed with her chin toward an empty table. Naomi carried her tray over and set it down beside the napkin holder.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: If the visitor is unsure, the visitor keeps gestures minimal and uses clear words instead of dramatic hand signals.

GPT-5.2.name: Sofia Andersson
GPT-5.2.story:
Sofia arrived at a coworking space in São Paulo and met a colleague, Renata, in the shared kitchen. People chatted near the espresso machine while a delivery rider waited by the entrance.

Renata asked, “How do you want to explain the new layout to the client?”

Sofia started to describe the changes, then stopped herself from making big hand shapes. She kept her hands close to her mug and spoke slowly. “First, we move the menu to the top,” she said. “Second, we shorten the signup form. Third, we add a clear button here.”

Renata nodded. “Show me where ‘here’ is.”

Sofia leaned toward the laptop and used one finger to indicate the corner of the screen instead of gesturing in the air. “This corner,” she said. “Next to the logo.”

A man at the counter greeted them with a wave and a quick thumbs-up. Sofia returned only a small nod.

Renata smiled. “You’re very precise.”

Sofia took a breath. “I’m not sure what gestures mean what everywhere,” she said, “so I try to be clear with words.”

Renata slid a chair out. Sofia sat down and opened the shared document, placing the cursor on the header area.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The visitor avoids obvious offensive gestures but uses suggestive gestures as jokes, undermining respect while appearing careful.

GPT-5.2.name: Liam O'Connor
GPT-5.2.story:
Liam joined a group of friends at a beach kiosk in Florianópolis, where they stood under a striped umbrella waiting for coconut water. He kept his hands tucked in his pockets at first and said, “I’m trying to be careful with gestures here.”

“Good,” Marina replied, laughing. “You’ll survive.”

When the vendor handed over the coconuts, Liam thanked him, then turned back to the group and made a small, suggestive hand motion as a joke, keeping it low near his waist.

Marina’s smile thinned. “Don’t,” she said.

Liam held up both palms. “Relax, it’s not offensive. I’m being careful,” he insisted, then repeated the same gesture with a grin.

A guy beside Marina looked down at his phone and didn’t respond. The vendor glanced over, then focused on cutting another coconut.

Marina said, “Just talk normally.”

Liam nodded quickly. “Sure, sure. I respect it.” He took a sip through the straw, then made one more suggestive motion toward a friend while saying, “I’m kidding, I’m kidding.”

Marina turned her body toward the water and started walking, and Liam followed behind her, still holding his coconut with both hands.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The visitor uses crude or aggressive gestures casually and refuses to adjust when told the gesture is inappropriate.

GPT-5.2.name: Derek Sullivan
GPT-5.2.story:
Derek walked with two new friends through a busy sidewalk market in Salvador, vendors calling out prices over piles of fruit. A motorbike squeezed past too close, and Derek jerked his arm up with an aggressive gesture toward the rider.

One of the friends, Pedro, touched Derek’s shoulder. “Hey, better not,” he said. “Don’t do that here.”

Derek shrugged and did it again, louder this time, adding, “He deserved it.”

Pedro’s mouth tightened. “That’s not a good sign to show people.”

Derek laughed and kept talking, punctuating his words with the same crude motion. “Where I’m from, this is nothing. People need to hear it.”

A woman selling acarajé glanced up from her pan and watched for a second. Two teenagers nearby slowed down, then kept walking.

Pedro tried again. “Seriously, just stop.”

Derek shook his head. “No. I’m not changing how I talk with my hands.” He pointed after the motorbike and repeated the gesture, then slapped his palm into his other hand as if closing the point.

Pedro stepped a half pace away, and Derek kept walking toward the next stall, still motioning as he spoke.
